questions.question: What is an ecosystem?
questions.questionThai: ระบบนิเวศคืออะไร?
options: A community of living things and non-living things working together
questions.explanation: An ecosystem is a community of living things and non-living things that interact with each other in an area.

questions.question: What are biotic factors?
questions.questionThai: ปัจจัยชีวภาพคืออะไร?
options: Non-living things like water and rocks
Living things like plants and animals
questions.explanation: Biotic factors are the living parts of an ecosystem, including plants, animals, fungi, and bacteria.

questions.question: What are abiotic factors?
questions.questionThai: ปัจจัยอชีวภาพคืออะไร?
options: Non-living things like water, sunlight, air, and soil
questions.explanation: Abiotic factors are the non-living parts of an ecosystem, including water, sunlight, air, soil, and temperature.

questions.question: What is a habitat?
questions.questionThai: ถิ่นที่อยู่คืออะไร?
options: The natural home where an organism lives
questions.explanation: A habitat is the natural home or environment where a plant or animal lives.

questions.question: What is a producer in an ecosystem?
questions.questionThai: ผู้ผลิตในระบบนิเวศคืออะไร?
options: An organism that makes its own food
questions.explanation: A producer is an organism that makes its own food, usually plants through photosynthesis.

questions.question: What is a consumer?
questions.questionThai: ผู้บริโภคคืออะไร?
options: An organism that eats other organisms
questions.explanation: A consumer is an organism that cannot make its own food and must eat other organisms.

questions.question: What is a decomposer?
questions.questionThai: ผู้ย่อยสลายคืออะไร?
options: An organism that breaks down dead plants and animals
questions.explanation: A decomposer is an organism that breaks down dead plants and animals, recycling nutrients back to the soil.

questions.question: Which is an example of an abiotic factor?
questions.questionThai: ข้อใดเป็นตัวอย่างของปัจจัยอชีวภาพ?
questions.explanation: Water is an abiotic (non-living) factor. Fish, trees, and bacteria are all biotic (living) factors.

questions.question: What is a population?
questions.questionThai: ประชากรคืออะไร?
options: All organisms of one species in an area
All different species in an area
questions.explanation: A population is all the organisms of one species living in a particular area.

questions.question: What is a community in ecology?
questions.questionThai: ชุมชนในนิเวศวิทยาคืออะไร?
options: All different species living together in an area
questions.explanation: A community is all the different species living together in a particular area.

questions.question: What happens when an ecosystem is balanced?
questions.questionThai: เกิดอะไรขึ้นเมื่อระบบนิเวศมีความสมดุล?
options: All parts work together and populations stay stable
questions.explanation: A balanced ecosystem has enough producers for consumers, nutrients are recycled, and populations stay relatively stable.

questions.question: In the reading passage, why are mangrove forests important for fish?
questions.questionThai: ในบทอ่าน ทำไมป่าชายเลนถึงสำคัญสำหรับปลา?
options: Mangrove roots provide safe places for baby fish to grow
questions.explanation: Mangrove roots provide safe hiding places for baby fish until they grow big enough to survive in open water.

questions.question: What would happen if all producers in an ecosystem died?
questions.questionThai: จะเกิดอะไรขึ้นถ้าผู้ผลิตทั้งหมดในระบบนิเวศตาย?
options: All consumers would eventually die because there would be no food source
questions.explanation: Producers are the base of the food chain. Without them, herbivores would have no food, then carnivores would have no food.

questions.question: Which is an example of a freshwater ecosystem?
questions.questionThai: ข้อใดเป็นตัวอย่างของระบบนิเวศน้ำจืด?
questions.explanation: A pond is a freshwater ecosystem. The ocean and coral reefs are saltwater (marine) ecosystems.

questions.question: Why are decomposers important in an ecosystem?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมผู้ย่อยสลายถึงสำคัญในระบบนิเวศ?
options: They recycle nutrients from dead organisms back to the soil
questions.explanation: Decomposers break down dead organisms and return nutrients to the soil so producers can use them to grow.

questions.question: What is the main source of energy for most ecosystems?
questions.questionThai: แหล่งพลังงานหลักสำหรับระบบนิเวศส่วนใหญ่คืออะไร?
questions.explanation: The sun is the main energy source. Producers capture sunlight through photosynthesis and convert it to food energy.

questions.question: Why did the pollution in the mangrove forest affect fish populations?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมมลพิษในป่าชายเลนถึงส่งผลต่อประชากรปลา?
options: Pollution killed mangrove trees, removing nursery habitat for baby fish
questions.explanation: When pollution killed mangrove trees, the fish lost their nursery habitat, affecting the whole ecosystem.

questions.question: What might happen if a predator is removed from an ecosystem?
questions.questionThai: จะเกิดอะไรขึ้นถ้าสัตว์นักล่าถูกนำออกจากระบบนิเวศ?
options: Prey populations could grow too large and eat too many plants, disrupting the ecosystem
questions.explanation: Without predators to control them, prey populations can grow too large and overeat plants, causing ecosystem imbalance.

questions.question: Why do different ecosystems have different types of organisms?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมระบบนิเวศต่างๆ ถึงมีสิ่งมีชีวิตประเภทต่างกัน?
options: All ecosystems have the same organisms
Different abiotic conditions require different adaptations to survive
questions.explanation: Different ecosystems have different temperatures, water availability, and other conditions, so organisms must have specific adaptations to survive there.

questions.question: How can one person help protect ecosystems?
questions.questionThai: คนหนึ่งคนสามารถช่วยปกป้องระบบนิเวศได้อย่างไร?
options: By reducing pollution, not littering, planting trees, and supporting conservation
questions.explanation: Individuals can help by reducing pollution, not littering, planting trees, conserving water, and supporting protected areas.
